title: Angular Basics
subtitle: Understand what Angular is and how it works
description: Learn the core ideas behind Angular тАФ what it is, how it compiles, its key building blocks, and how to write your very first component.
keyPoints: Angular is a TypeScript-based framework for building Single Page Applications (SPAs)
Every Angular app is built from Components тАФ TypeScript class + HTML template + optional styles
The @Component decorator configures how a component looks and behaves
Angular 17+ uses Standalone Components тАФ no NgModules required
Angular compiles templates at build time for maximum performance
The CLI creates a production-ready project in one command

sections.title: What is Angular?
sections.content:
Angular is an open-source, TypeScript-based web framework built and maintained by Google. It is used to build Single Page Applications (SPAs) тАФ web apps that load once in the browser and then dynamically update the view without full page reloads.

Angular provides a complete, opinionated solution: routing, forms, HTTP, animations, testing тАФ all built in. This makes it ideal for large, enterprise-scale applications where consistency and maintainability matter.

Angular (v2+) is completely different from AngularJS (v1). AngularJS used JavaScript and a digest cycle. Angular uses TypeScript, Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation, and a much faster change detection system based on Zone.js (and now Signals).

sections.title: Angular Architecture at a Glance
sections.content:
An Angular application is a tree of Components. Each component controls a section of the UI. Components communicate through Inputs (parent тЖТ child), Outputs (child тЖТ parent), and Services (shared state/logic).

The key building blocks are:
тАв Components тАФ the UI building blocks (class + template + styles)
тАв Templates тАФ HTML extended with Angular syntax
тАв Directives тАФ extend HTML with custom behavior
тАв Services тАФ reusable business logic and shared state
тАв Dependency Injection тАФ Angular's system for providing services to components
тАв Router тАФ navigates between views without page reloads

sections.caption: Components form a tree. Services are injected via Angular's DI system.

sections.title: Your First Angular Component
sections.content:
A component is just a TypeScript class decorated with @Component. The decorator tells Angular:
тАв What HTML tag to use (selector)
тАв What the template looks like (template or templateUrl)
тАв What styles to apply (styles or styleUrl)

sections.title: Minimal Component
sections.explanation: The @Component decorator is what turns a plain TypeScript class into an Angular component. The selector defines the custom HTML tag you'll use to render it.

sections.title: Using the Component
sections.explanation: You use a component the same way you use a native HTML element тАФ just with your custom selector tag.

sections.content: Angular 17+ defaults all components to standalone: true. You no longer need NgModules (AppModule, SharedModule, etc.). Each component declares its own imports[] тАФ the other components, directives, and pipes it needs.

sections.title: Angular Project Structure
sections.content: When you run 'ng new my-app', Angular CLI generates a well-structured project. Understanding this structure is key to navigating any Angular codebase.

sections.title: Generated Project Structure
sections.code:
my-app/
тФЬтФАтФА src/
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА app/
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА app.ts           тЖР Root component class
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА app.html         тЖР Root component template
тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА app.routes.ts    тЖР Route definitions
тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА app.config.ts    тЖР App-level providers (DI)
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА main.ts              тЖР Entry point: bootstraps the app
тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА styles.scss          тЖР Global styles
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА index.html           тЖР Single HTML file loaded by browser
тФЬтФАтФА public/                  тЖР Static assets (images, fonts, JSON)
тФЬтФАтФА angular.json             тЖР CLI build configuration
тФЬтФАтФА tsconfig.json            тЖР TypeScript configuration
тФФтФАтФА package.json             тЖР Dependencies

sections.title: main.ts тАФ The Entry Point
sections.explanation: main.ts is the single entry point. Angular compiles everything from here. In production, the entire app becomes optimized static files.

sections.content: Never put business logic in main.ts. It should only bootstrap the app. All your code belongs inside the src/app/ folder, organized into features, core, and shared.

sections.title: How Angular Compiles Your App
sections.content:
Angular uses Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation. This means Angular compiles your templates to optimized JavaScript at BUILD TIME тАФ not in the browser at runtime.

Benefits:
тАв Faster rendering (no template parsing in browser)
тАв Template errors caught at build time (not runtime)
тАв Smaller bundle (Angular compiler not shipped to browser)
тАв Better security (templates already compiled)

sections.title: CLI Commands You'll Use Daily
sections.code:
# Create a new project
ng new my-app --standalone

# Start development server (live reload)
ng serve

# Generate a new component
ng generate component features/home
# or shorthand:
ng g c features/home

# Generate a service
ng g s core/services/auth

# Build for production
ng build

# Run tests
ng test

interviewQuestions.a: Angular is an open-source, TypeScript-based web framework developed and maintained by Google. It is used to build Single Page Applications (SPAs) that load once and dynamically update without full page reloads. Angular provides a complete solution including routing, forms, HTTP client, animations, and testing tools.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the difference between Angular and AngularJS?
interviewQuestions.a: AngularJS (v1) was released in 2010 and used JavaScript with a digest cycle for change detection. Angular (v2+, released 2016) is a complete rewrite using TypeScript, component-based architecture, Ahead-of-Time compilation, and a hierarchical Dependency Injection system. They are fundamentally different frameworks.

interviewQuestions.q: What is a Component in Angular?
interviewQuestions.a: A Component is the fundamental building block of an Angular application. It consists of: (1) a TypeScript class with the business logic, (2) an HTML template that defines the view, and (3) optional CSS styles. It is decorated with @Component which configures the selector, template, and styles.

The @Component decorator is a function from @angular/core that marks a TypeScript class as an Angular component. It accepts a metadata object with: selector (the HTML tag name), template or templateUrl (the view), styles or styleUrl (the CSS), and imports[] (other standalone components/directives it uses).

interviewQuestions.q: What is AOT compilation in Angular?
interviewQuestions.a: Ahead-of-Time (AOT) compilation converts Angular HTML templates and TypeScript code into efficient JavaScript at BUILD TIME, before the browser loads the app. Benefits include: faster rendering (no template compilation in browser), catching template errors at build time, smaller bundles, and better security.

Standalone Components (introduced in Angular 14, default in Angular 17+) are components that don't require an NgModule. They declare their own dependencies in the imports[] array of the @Component decorator. This simplifies the architecture and makes components more self-contained and portable.

interviewQuestions.q: What is the purpose of main.ts?
interviewQuestions.a: main.ts is the entry point of an Angular application. It calls bootstrapApplication() with the root component (usually AppComponent) and the application configuration (appConfig). This starts the Angular framework, initializes all providers, and renders the root component into the DOM.
